frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Observer integrates sensory evidence over an adjustable temporal window.

frameworks.name: perceptual confidence
frameworks.one_line: Confidence read-out uses the same task-adapted timescale as the decision.

frameworks.name: change detection
frameworks.one_line: Auditory change-point task probes timescale of evidence weighting.

core_question: Do people adapt the timescale of evidence evaluation for both decisions and confidence to the expected signal duration of the task?
key_assumptions: Decisions reflect temporal integration of relevant evidence
Confidence is derived from the same evidence stream as the decision
Task context can shift the integration window
manipulations: Expected signal duration (short vs longer) across contexts
Auditory change-detection setting (extending prior visual results)
key_findings: Shorter signal-duration contexts yield shorter evidence-evaluation timescales for change detection
Confidence judgments also adopt correspondingly shorter timescales in short-duration contexts
Effects hold in model-free analyses and generalize to the auditory domain
interpretations: Both decision and confidence evidence weighting are flexibly tuned to task demands
